children.heading: Marine Mammal Protection Act
children.label: a Marine Mammal Protection Act
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce has authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 1361

children.heading: Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
children.label: b Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act

children.text: The Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 1801
The Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Reauthorization Act of 2006 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 1891
The High Seas Driftnet Fishing Moratorium Protection Act ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 1826d
The Shark Conservation Act of 2010 ( note; 1857 note).16 U.S.C. 1826k
The Shark Finning Prohibition Act ( note).16 U.S.C. 1822

children.heading: Fishermen’s Protective Act of 1967
children.label: c Fishermen’s Protective Act of 1967
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce and Secretary of the Interior have authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under section 8 of the Fishermen’s Protective Act of 1967 ().22 U.S.C. 1978

children.heading: Agreement on Port State Measures To Prevent, Deter and Eliminate Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated Fishing
children.label: d Agreement on Port State Measures To Prevent, Deter and Eliminate Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated Fishing
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce has authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under the Port State Measures Agreement Act of 2015 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 7401

children.heading: Endangered Species Act
children.label: e Endangered Species Act
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture, the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of Homeland Security, the Secretary of Commerce, and the Secretary of the Treasury have authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under the Endangered Species Act of 1973 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 1531

children.heading: Lacey Act
children.label: f Lacey Act
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture, the Secretary of Commerce, the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the Secretary of the Treasury have authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 3371

children.heading: Migratory Bird Treaty Act
children.label: g Migratory Bird Treaty Act
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior has authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under the Migratory Bird Treaty Act of 1918 ( et seq.).116 U.S.C. 703See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Eliminate, Neutralize, and Disrupt Wildlife Trafficking Act
children.label: h Eliminate, Neutralize, and Disrupt Wildlife Trafficking Act
children.text: The Secretary of State, the Secretary of the Interior, the Attorney General, and Administrator of the United States Agency for International Development have authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under the Eliminate, Neutralize, and Disrupt Wildlife Trafficking Act of 2016 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 7601

children.heading: Wild Bird Conservation Act
children.label: i Wild Bird Conservation Act
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior has authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under the Wild Bird Conservation Act of 1992 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 4901

children.heading: Customs seizure and other authorities
children.label: j Customs seizure and other authorities
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security has authority to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under or .section 1499 of this titlesection 1595a of this title

children.heading: Other relevant provisions of law
children.label: k Other relevant provisions of law
children.text: The Interagency Environment Committee may request the heads of other Federal agencies to take appropriate monitoring or enforcement actions under other relevant provisions of law.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: l Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to supersede or otherwise limit in any manner the functions or authority of the head of any Federal agency described in this section under any other provision of law.
